input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard – standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml file for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me create this directly - no need to explore a codebase since this is a new app.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page accessible from flyout
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage (navigated to programmatically)

Let me create both AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"

    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard – standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml** defines three flyout items:
- **Dashboard** — single `ShellContent`, opens directly.
- **Products** — contains two `<Tab>` children (`Active` / `Archived`), which render as bottom tabs when you select this flyout item.
- **Settings** — another single-page flyout item.

**AppShell.xaml.cs** registers `ProductDetailsPage` via `Routing.RegisterRoute`. Because it's not in the visual hierarchy, you navigate to it programmatically:

```csharp
// From any products page, pass the product ID as a query param:
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

Then receive it in `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` or by implementing `IQueryAttributable`.

**Pages you still need to create** under a `Pages/` folder:
`DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, `ProductDetailsPage`.
